title: Route replay and season-wrap phase
summary: Twenty-seventh structured packet for Eco Explorer. After the three-route station pass, the best next phase is not a fourth live route. The current three-route season should instead become more replayable and more chapter-like through route-aware revisit variants, one compact season-wrap station pass, and quiet coastal corridor-authored beats that mirror the inland continuity work.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a route-aware revisit variant matrix using day-part, weather, phenology, process moments, notes, and nursery clue windows.
execution_order.why_now: The current world already has enough systems to make repeat outings feel richer without adding another feature lane.

execution_order.goal: Implement the first route-aware revisit variant pass.
execution_order.why_now: Replay value is the highest-leverage improvement now that three routes are live.

execution_order.goal: Review the revisit variants for subtlety, readability, and chapter feel.
execution_order.why_now: Replay support only helps if it feels authored instead of random.

execution_order.goal: Prepare compact season-wrap copy and station-flow guidance.
execution_order.why_now: The station needs a slightly stronger sense of closure without becoming a score screen.

execution_order.goal: Add one compact season-wrap and next-outing teaser pass to the field station.
execution_order.why_now: A better stopping point will make the routes feel more like episodes.

execution_order.goal: Review the season-wrap pass for warmth, restraint, and clarity.
execution_order.why_now: The station should feel more satisfying, not busier.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the coastal corridor authored-beat matrix.
execution_order.why_now: The coast should get the same authored continuity the inland branch now has.

execution_order.goal: Add quiet authored coastal corridor beats.
execution_order.why_now: The full chain will feel more coherent if the coast carries the same subtle continuity the inland seams already have.

execution_order.goal: Review the coastal corridor continuity pass.
execution_order.why_now: The coast should feel authored and calm, not mechanically busier.

execution_order.goal: Optional small follow-on to tighten route replay surfacing if the phase reviews cleanly.
execution_order.why_now: Keep one small buffer item behind the continuity wave instead of letting the queue go empty again.

guardrails: Do not add a fourth live route in this phase.
Do not add a heavier seasonal calendar or management screen.
Keep season-wrap UI notebook-like and avoid score-screen energy.
Keep coastal corridor beats quiet and carrier-first.
Use existing living-world seams instead of inventing a new replay system.
acceptance_focus: Replay support makes the current three-route season feel richer without adding a fourth route.
Station stops feel more like episode endings and next-outing handoffs.
The coastal half of the world gains the same authored continuity feel as the inland half.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-route-replay-and-season-wrap-phase.md, and this packet. Treat the current three-route field season as the live baseline. The next phase should make it more replayable and more chapter-like through revisit variants, a compact season-wrap pass, and quiet coastal corridor continuity.
